title: podman 2.2.1 seccomp=unconfined behaves differently for `build` vs `run`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

On Centos 8 (and likely RHEL),  I found podman running a Fedora 34 (beta) image interactively, will return false (exit 1) for `/usr/bin/test -r /root/anaconda-ks.cfg` (`ls` and `stat` both work fine).  This is suspected to be caused/fixed by https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1927635  I'm able to work around this by using `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined`.  However, this does not work for `podman build`, `test -r` continues to incorrectly exit 1 for an existing file.
Curiously, running `buildah bud --security-opt seccomp=unconfined` (1.16.7) does ***not*** exhibit this behavior (i.e. it works fine).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Using Containerfile:
   ```
   FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
   RUN ls -la /root && \
       test -r /root/anaconda-ks.cfg
   ```
2. `podman build --security-opt seccomp=unconfined .`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman build --security-opt seccomp=unconfined .
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2a5c98b3ee0d done
Copying config f6343ff3cc done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN ls -la /root &&     test -r /root/anaconda-ks.cfg
total 36
dr-xr-x---.  2 root root  196 Feb 22 09:57 .
dr-xr-xr-x. 18 root root    6 Feb 24 19:40 ..
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root   18 Jan 28 11:17 .bash_logout
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  141 Jan 28 11:17 .bash_profile
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  429 Jan 28 11:17 .bashrc
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  100 Jan 28 11:17 .cshrc
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  129 Jan 28 11:17 .tcshrc
-rw-------.  1 root root 3453 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-ks.cfg
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root 2162 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-post-nochroot.log
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  237 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-post.log
-rw-------.  1 root root 3227 Feb 22 09:57 original-ks.cfg
Error: error building at STEP "RUN ls -la /root &&     test -r /root/anaconda-ks.cfg": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ podman rmi -af
$ buildah bud --security-opt seccomp=unconfined .
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2a5c98b3ee0d done
Copying config f6343ff3cc done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN ls -la /root &&     test -r /root/anaconda-ks.cfg
total 36
dr-xr-x---.  2 root root  196 Feb 22 09:57 .
drwxr-xr-x. 18 root root    6 Feb 24 19:42 ..
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root   18 Jan 28 11:17 .bash_logout
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  141 Jan 28 11:17 .bash_profile
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  429 Jan 28 11:17 .bashrc
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  100 Jan 28 11:17 .cshrc
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  129 Jan 28 11:17 .tcshrc
-rw-------.  1 root root 3453 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-ks.cfg
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root 2162 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-post-nochroot.log
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root  237 Feb 22 09:57 anaconda-post.log
-rw-------.  1 root root 3227 Feb 22 09:57 original-ks.cfg
STEP 3: COMMIT testing
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob fe5afcdceb03 skipped: already exists
Copying blob b506ae15b507 done
Copying config 3d25b2c543 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 3d25b2c5433
3d25b2c543309adf71f12bbadfe394d8a623d4258e9d001a76251d7d94c7488a
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The symptoms reproduce for both root and rootless podman.  The problem **does not** reproduce using a `fedora:33` (or earlier) or `ubi8` image.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

API Version:  2
Go Version:   go1.14.12

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Reproduced on a freshly installed and updated CentOS 8 VM

comments.body: ***Clarification:***  The issue here isn't about F34 or CentOS specifically, it's that `podman run` and `podman build` behave differently WRT `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined`
Different crun version in use on 33 vs 34, maybe?

That must be the seccomp farts (https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1927635) coming from newer versions of glibc attempting to use new syscalls with libseccomp throwing EPERMs.

I think we can close the issue since there's nothing Podman can do at the moment.
@vrothberg @mheon how does any of that affect processing of the `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` command-line option?  This bug is about different behavior for `run` vs `build`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I can not see any difference in handling between the two I am examined the code of the two, and tested by removing the getcwd syscall, and both fail/succeed in the same way. So I am going to close this issue. If you have a reproducer I would love to look at it.

@rhatdan I have exactly the same issue with podman 3.0.1.
See https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3776
